skill.name: istio-traffic-management
skill.description: Configure Istio traffic management including routing, load balancing, circuit breakers, and canary deployments. Use when implementing service mesh traffic policies, progressive delivery, or resilience patterns in Kubernetes environments.
skill.summary: Expert guide for Istio service mesh traffic management with production-ready templates
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only YAML configuration templates and documentation for Istio traffic management. The 'external commands' flagged are bash commands in markdown documentation blocks (istioctl commands). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings are triggered by Istio API version strings (v1beta1) in YAML headers, not actual crypto. The 'hardcoded URLs' are legitimate links to official Istio documentation. No executable code, no security risks, no prompt injection attempts detected.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: All 44 detected patterns are false positives from documentation and YAML templates. The 'external_commands' patterns are bash commands in markdown code blocks (istioctl). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns are Istio API version strings (v1beta1). The 'hardcoded URLs' are official documentation links.

content.user_title: Manage Istio Traffic Like a Pro
content.value_statement: Simplify Istio service mesh configuration with production-ready templates for routing, canary deployments, circuit breakers, and traffic shifting. Eliminate trial-and-error with proven patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate VirtualService and DestinationRule YAML configurations
Implement canary and blue-green deployment strategies
Configure circuit breakers, retries, and timeouts
Set up traffic mirroring for testing environments
Create ingress gateway configurations with TLS
Apply load balancing strategies including consistent hashing
limitations: Requires existing Istio installation in Kubernetes cluster
Does not install or configure Istio itself
Templates focus on traffic management only (not security or telemetry policies)
Assumes familiarity with Kubernetes and basic service mesh concepts

use_cases.title: Platform Engineer Setting Up Canary Deployments
use_cases.description: Configure gradual traffic rollout from stable to canary versions with weighted routing and automatic rollback on failures.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Implementing Resilience Patterns
use_cases.description: Add circuit breakers, retries, and timeouts to prevent cascading failures and improve service reliability.

use_cases.title: SRE Debugging Traffic Routing Issues
use_cases.description: Use istioctl commands to analyze and validate VirtualService and DestinationRule configurations in production clusters.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Service Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a VirtualService for my-service that routes traffic based on the x-user header. Send users with header value 'beta' to v2 subset, all others to v1.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple header-based routing for A/B testing

prompt_templates.title: Configure Canary Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a canary deployment for payment-service. Route 95% of traffic to stable version, 5% to canary. Include DestinationRule with both subsets.
prompt_templates.scenario: Progressive rollout with small traffic percentage

prompt_templates.title: Add Circuit Breaker Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DestinationRule for api-service with circuit breaker settings: max 100 connections, 1000 HTTP requests, eject pods after 5 consecutive errors, 30s base ejection time.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Traffic Mirroring
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up traffic mirroring from production to staging. Mirror 100% of requests to v2 subset for testing without affecting live traffic.
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing new versions with real production traffic

output_examples.input: Create a canary deployment with 10% traffic
output_examples.output: Generates VirtualService with weight-based routing (90% stable, 10% canary) and DestinationRule defining both subsets with version labels

output_examples.input: Add retries and timeout to orders service
output_examples.output: Creates VirtualService with 3 retry attempts, 3s per-try timeout, and 10s overall timeout for the orders host

output_examples.input: Set up circuit breaker for checkout service
output_examples.output: Generates DestinationRule with connection pool limits and outlier detection including consecutive5xxErrors and baseEjectionTime

best_practices: Always test Istio configurations with istioctl analyze before applying to production
Start with simple VirtualService rules and add complexity incrementally
Monitor traffic patterns with Kiali dashboards after deploying new routing rules
Use meaningful subset names (stable, canary, v1, v2) and label services consistently
anti_patterns: Do not configure retries without limits - this can cause cascading failures
Avoid mirroring traffic to production databases - mirror to test environments only
Never skip subset validation in DestinationRules - undefined subsets cause routing failures
Do not use 100% canary weight immediately - always start with small percentages

faq.question: What is the difference between VirtualService and DestinationRule?
faq.answer: VirtualService controls how traffic is routed to destinations (routing rules). DestinationRule defines policies applied after routing (load balancing, circuit breakers, subsets).

faq.question: How do I gradually increase canary traffic?
faq.answer: Update the weight values in VirtualService incrementally (5%, 10%, 25%, 50%, 100%). Monitor metrics at each step before proceeding.

faq.question: Why is my traffic not reaching the canary subset?
faq.answer: Verify pods have the correct version labels matching DestinationRule subsets. Check istioctl proxy-config endpoints to see which endpoints are registered.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Istio installed?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides Istio configuration templates. You must have Istio installed in your Kubernetes cluster before applying these resources.

faq.question: How do I debug routing issues?
faq.answer: Use istioctl analyze to validate configuration, istioctl proxy-config routes to view effective routes, and istioctl proxy-config log to enable debug logging.

faq.question: What is traffic mirroring used for?
faq.answer: Traffic mirroring sends copies of live requests to a mirror service (typically v2 or staging) for testing without affecting real users. The mirror responses are ignored.
